titleStmt.title: Letter from the Chief Secretary’s Office to George K. Sherlock, 10 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Chief Secretary’s Office to George K. Sherlock, Sessional Crown Solicitor for County Cork, West Riding. James Campbell (1851-1931), Attorney-General for Ireland, 1916 This letter advises Mr. Sherlock that the Attorney-General has approved his application for leave of absence, nominating his partner in practice as his substitute.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13783

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10th August 1916
Leave of Absence Dear Sir, The Attorney General consents to your having the leave asked
for. Yours truly, Grattan K Sherlock Esq, S.C. Solicitor, Bandon
Law Chambers Bandon 8th August 1916 I George K. Sherlock, Sessional
Crown Solicitor for County Cork,
West Riding beg to apply for leave of absence
from the 18 th day of August 1916 to the 20th
day of September 1916. George K Sherlock Leas Mooreson
WR Co. Cork To The Right Honourbale, Proposed Substitute
Robert Webb Sherlock
Solicitor. Qualification
Is my partner. Was sworn in
as a solicitor in 1889 and he
has for several years back acted
for me as a Substitute with the
consent of the Attorney General
at vacation time. Crown case pending on which the
Sessl. Crown solicitor has been ordered to take
charge of which may possibly come on during his absence. Title of Cause Nature of Case
Nil
